title: Anarchy
content:
I saw a city filled with lust and shame,
             Where men, like wolves, slunk through the grim half-light;
            And sudden, in the midst of it, there came
             One who spoke boldly for the cause of Right.

            And speaking, fell before that brutish race
             Like some poor wren that shrieking eagles tear,
            While brute Dishonour, with her bloodless face
             Stood by and smote his lips that moved in prayer.

            "Speak not of God!    In centuries that word
             Hath not been uttered!    Our own king are we."
            And God stretched forth his finger as He heard
             And o'er it cast a thousand leagues of sea.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Alexander McCrae, titled "Anarchy", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
